insight: For Egypt, hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup have had a mixed impact on their momentum. On average, they tend to lose a bit of momentum after a break, with a mean change of -0.050 per minute, although they did manage to gain momentum in one out of the two breaks. When Egypt was pressing before a break, they struggled to maintain their intensity, losing momentum at a rate of -0.200 per minute and failing to keep their momentum after the break. In contrast, when they weren't pressing before a break, they actually tended to gain momentum at a rate of +0.100 per minute, suggesting that breaks can provide a brief respite and allow them to regroup and refocus.
